response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer (15+ years, CS degree, strong testing culture, and genuine high-scale experience handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), but the resume shows no experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, growth engineering, or metrics/telemetry platform work — the core domain of this role. There is also no evidence of Principal/IC6-level scope such as leading multi-workstream initiatives across teams at enterprise scale; his trajectory is senior/staff IC work at small-to-mid-size companies. Recent AI-agent tooling and MCP work aligns with a preferred qualification, but the domain and level gaps outweigh it for a principal-level experimentation-systems role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, backend, mobile, and embedded, with a CS degree and polyglot proficiency (TypeScript, C/C++, Java, D)
Real production-scale experience: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second, plus async I/O and caching expertise
Recent hands-on AI tooling work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) matching the preferred AI-velocity qualification
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, telemetry, or growth-measurement platform experience — the central requirement of the role
No demonstrated principal-level scope: no evidence of architecting systems used across multiple teams or leading multi-workstream, cross-org initiatives
No visible statistical analysis or data-science-adjacent background (metrics design, bias detection, experiment analysis)

key_factors.factor: Level gap between senior/staff IC background and Principal IC6 scope
Strong engineering fundamentals with proven high-scale systems work
